title: MGB researchers just developed a new risk calculator for obesity. Here’s why that’s a game changer.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mass General Brigham researchers developed a genetic risk calculator predicting obesity and Type 2 diabetes decades before onset.
The tool uses a vast global dataset to improve risk prediction across multiple populations, outperforming existing models.
It forecasts obesity-related health outcomes, aiding early intervention strategies, and highlights significant genetic influences.

title: Why this ICU nurse is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma recovery
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Erin Daniel, a 25-year-old ICU nurse at Brigham and Women's Hospital, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Daniel runs to support the Stepping Strong Foundation, inspired by trauma survivor Gillian Reny's recovery journey.
The Stepping Strong mission advances trauma research, aiming to improve patient recovery outcomes and restore mobility.

title: Poll: Mass. Legislature underwater in court of public opinion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Only 28% of Massachusetts residents view the state Legislature favorably, with 51% holding an unfavorable view, according to a recent poll.
87% of respondents prioritize government transparency, supporting proposed measures to make legislative records publicly accessible under state law.
83% believe the Legislature should allow a long-delayed audit by State Auditor Diana DiZoglio, despite legal challenges and opposition.

title: Police seek Mass. vehicle after driver involved in fatal Providence hit-and-run
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Providence police seek a black Chevrolet Equinox with a Massachusetts plate involved in a fatal hit-and-run.
Kevin Chappie, 46, was killed on Corliss Street while waiting for a rideshare; vehicle fled the scene.
Public urged to contact Providence Police Traffic Bureau with information on the unresolved incident.

title: Martha Stewart’s The Bedford is now open at this New England resort
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Martha Stewart's The Bedford opened at Foxwoods Resort Casino in Connecticut on March 14, 2026.
The restaurant offers signature dishes like a $23 bread basket, pierogies, and exclusive Foxwoods dishes.
The Bedford spans 10,000 square feet, seating 204 guests, with Martha-tinis and wine at the bar.

title: Weekend commuter rail disruption will now leak into Fridays
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Weekend commuter rail shutdowns at North Station will extend to include several Fridays, impacting travel on specific lines.
Shuttle buses will replace train services on lowell, Newburyport/Rockport, and Fitchburg lines due to signal upgrades.
MBTA's signal modernization project at Tower A is aimed at improving North Station services, expected to complete by summer.

title: This Massachusetts commute ranks as the most stressful, poll finds
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Zakim Bridge/I-93 North in Boston was ranked as Massachusetts' most stressful commute.
A poll of over 3,000 US workers identified the Southeast Expressway and I-95/Route 128 in Waltham as other frustrating routes.
Boston appeared frequently in national rankings alongside New York, Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Philadelphia for difficult commutes.

title: Jaylen Brown describes ‘surreal’ experience at his first St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown attended his first St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, streaming the experience on Twitch.
Brown enjoyed the parade chants and compared them to MVP chants he received for scoring 41 points against the Suns.
Brown is averaging 28.4 points, 7.1 rebounds, and 5.1 assists, helping Celtics maintain second place in the Eastern Conference.

title: Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Essex Police Department evacuated after resident brought in a suspected unexploded mortar shell found on their property.
The bomb squad determined the item was a harmless, empty fuel canister resembling an old mortar shell.
Police urge not to move suspected explosives and to call 911 if such items are discovered.

title: Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest offers one-way flights starting at $59 for travel from April 7 to June 10.
The sale runs until March 19, with deals available for Tuesday and Wednesday flights.
Flights from Boston include $59 to Baltimore, $94 to Cleveland, and $160 to San Diego.
